channel.title: Writings on Joseph C
channel.description: Recent content in Writings on Joseph C

item.title: On being ok with other people being at different stages in their lives than you
item.description: Much like how light can be a particle or a wave, other people’s life trajectories can be both parallel to yours and inhabit a completely different dimension. Since reading this article called ‘Instead of your life’s purpose’, I’ve been considering a new perspective of life being non-linear — life that doesn’t move in a straight line, is not scalable and is not an easily dividable experience. Accepting this perspective has eased a lot of rigidity that I’d been feeling, days flow a bit easier now and there’s more space for meaning to shine through.

item.title: drawing decisions
item.description: This is a photo I took about three years ago of the Moon using a digital camera with a slow shutter speed. The camera acted as a sort of a paintbrush, where I moved the light from the Moon that came into contact with the camera lens like ink along a blank page. The shutter lasted about two seconds which I set because I wanted time to ‘draw with the Moon’ using the camera but didn’t want to let too much light in and loose the contrast of the bright moon against the night sky.

item.title: starfish.systems
item.description: ::starfish.systems activated :: A tiny, experimental design studio exploring the realms of spacial interfaces, virtual spaces and the transference of visual information through digital applications.
